title: Chapter 6 - Collaboration: Pull Requests, Reviews, and Issues
description: Check your understanding of GitHub collaboration features.

questions.prompt: What is a pull request (PR) used for?

options.label: Request to merge changes from one branch into another
Request to delete someone else's repository

explanations.overview: PRs help teams review changes before merging into important branches like main.

byOptionId.a: Correct. A PR proposes merging changes.
byOptionId.b: Incorrect. PRs are not deletion requests.
byOptionId.c: Incorrect. PRs do not rename tools.
byOptionId.d: Incorrect. Passwords should not be stored in repos.

options.label: They force Git to run faster
They can catch mistakes and improve readability
They replace the need for testing

explanations.overview: Reviews add another set of eyes and help keep code quality high.

byOptionId.a: Incorrect. Reviews do not affect Git speed.
byOptionId.b: Incorrect. Reviews do not permanently block merging.
byOptionId.c: Correct. Reviews catch bugs and improve code quality.
byOptionId.d: Incorrect. Reviews help, but testing is still important.

questions.prompt: On GitHub, what are Issues commonly used for?

options.label: Tracking bugs, tasks, and feature ideas

explanations.overview: Issues are a project planning and discussion tool for work items.

byOptionId.a: Incorrect. Compiled output should usually not be stored in issues.
byOptionId.b: Correct. Issues track work and discussions.
byOptionId.c: Incorrect. Issues are not an encryption feature.
byOptionId.d: Incorrect. Repo privacy is a settings choice.

questions.prompt: What is a fork on GitHub?

options.label: Your own copy of someone else's repository on GitHub
A way to delete all branches
A Git command that stages files

explanations.overview: Forks let you work on a repo when you do not have direct write access to the original.

byOptionId.a: Correct. A fork is your copy on GitHub.
byOptionId.b: Incorrect. Forking does not delete branches.
byOptionId.c: Incorrect. It is not a commit message type.
